tasks.subject: [site-feature-category-pages] [P1] Add media fields to pulse-scheduler, relay-message-bus, and mesh-topology in features.ts
tasks.description:
Add `media` fields to exactly 3 features in `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/lib/features.ts`. The `Feature` interface already has an optional `media?: { screenshot?: string; demoUrl?: string; alt?: string; }` field — no type changes are needed.

Add the following `media` entries to the three named features (all other fields remain unchanged):

1. `pulse-scheduler` (currently around line 192 in features.ts, inside the PULSE section):
```typescript
  media: {
    screenshot: '/features/pulse-scheduler.png',
    alt: 'Pulse Scheduler — visual cron builder with run history panel',
  },
```

2. `relay-message-bus` (currently around line 213, inside the RELAY section):
```typescript
  media: {
    screenshot: '/features/relay-message-bus.png',
    alt: 'Relay Message Bus — activity feed showing inter-agent message routing',
  },
```

3. `mesh-topology` (currently around line 296, inside the MESH section):
```typescript
  media: {
    screenshot: '/features/mesh-topology.png',
    alt: 'Mesh Topology Graph — interactive force-directed graph of agents and adapters',
  },
```

Each `media` object should be added as a new field inside the feature object literal, placed after the `docsUrl` field and before `relatedFeatures` (matching the field ordering established by the interface definition in the same file).

No other changes to `features.ts` are needed. Do not modify any other features. Do not change any existing fields on these three features.

Acceptance criteria:
- `pulse-scheduler` has `media.screenshot === '/features/pulse-scheduler.png'` and `media.alt` set
- `relay-message-bus` has `media.screenshot === '/features/relay-message-bus.png'` and `media.alt` set
- `mesh-topology` has `media.screenshot === '/features/mesh-topology.png'` and `media.alt` set
- All other 10 features remain unchanged
- `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- `pnpm lint` passes with zero warnings
tasks.activeForm: Adding media fields to feature data

tasks.subject: [site-feature-category-pages] [P1] Create placeholder SVG files at PNG paths in apps/site/public/features/
tasks.description:
Create the `apps/site/public/features/` directory and place three placeholder image files inside it. These files are SVG content saved with `.png` filenames — Next.js serves them as static assets from `/public`.

Each file is a 1600×900 SVG with:
- Background fill: `#E8E3D8` (cream, matching the site palette)
- Centered text in `#2C2C2C` (charcoal) at 50% opacity
- Font: monospace
- Text positioned at x=800, y=450 with `text-anchor="middle"` and `dominant-baseline="middle"`

Create `apps/site/public/features/pulse-scheduler.png`:
```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1600" height="900" viewBox="0 0 1600 900">
  <rect width="1600" height="900" fill="#E8E3D8"/>
  <text x="800" y="450" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="middle"
        font-family="monospace" font-size="32" fill="#2C2C2C" opacity="0.5">
    Pulse Scheduler
  </text>
</svg>
```

Create `apps/site/public/features/relay-message-bus.png`:
```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1600" height="900" viewBox="0 0 1600 900">
  <rect width="1600" height="900" fill="#E8E3D8"/>
  <text x="800" y="450" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="middle"
        font-family="monospace" font-size="32" fill="#2C2C2C" opacity="0.5">
    Relay Message Bus
  </text>
</svg>
```

Create `apps/site/public/features/mesh-topology.png`:
```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1600" height="900" viewBox="0 0 1600 900">
  <rect width="1600" height="900" fill="#E8E3D8"/>
  <text x="800" y="450" text-anchor="middle" dominant-baseline="middle"
        font-family="monospace" font-size="32" fill="#2C2C2C" opacity="0.5">
    Mesh Topology Graph
  </text>
</svg>
```

Acceptance criteria:
- `apps/site/public/features/` directory exists
- `pulse-scheduler.png`, `relay-message-bus.png`, and `mesh-topology.png` all exist in that directory
- Each file is valid SVG content at 1600×900
- File sizes are minimal (~300 bytes each)
- Next.js serves them as static assets at `/features/pulse-scheduler.png`, `/features/relay-message-bus.png`, `/features/mesh-topology.png`

tasks.subject: [site-feature-category-pages] [P3] Add category pages to sitemap.ts at priority 0.6
tasks.description:
Update `apps/site/src/app/sitemap.ts` to include the 8 feature category pages in the sitemap at priority 0.6.

The existing sitemap.ts imports `features` from `@/layers/features/marketing`. Add `CATEGORY_LABELS` and `FeatureCategory` to that import:

```typescript
import { features, CATEGORY_LABELS, type FeatureCategory } from '@/layers/features/marketing';
```

Then add a new `featureCategoryPages` variable after the existing `featurePages` variable:

```typescript
const featureCategoryPages: MetadataRoute.Sitemap = (
  Object.keys(CATEGORY_LABELS) as FeatureCategory[]
).map((category) => ({
  url: `${BASE_URL}/features/category/${category}`,
  changeFrequency: 'monthly' as const,
  priority: 0.6,
}));
```

Then update the return statement to include `featureCategoryPages` between `featurePages` and `docPages`:

```typescript
return [...staticPages, ...featureCatalogPage, ...featurePages, ...featureCategoryPages, ...docPages, ...blogPages];
```

The priority hierarchy after this change:
- `/features/{slug}` → 0.8
- `/features` → 0.7
- `/features/category/{category}` → 0.6 (new)
- blog pages → 0.6
- doc pages → 0.7

No other changes to `sitemap.ts` are needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- `sitemap.ts` imports `CATEGORY_LABELS` and `FeatureCategory` from `@/layers/features/marketing`
- `featureCategoryPages` array is built from `Object.keys(CATEGORY_LABELS)` — produces exactly 8 entries
- All 8 entries use `url: \`${BASE_URL}/features/category/${category}\`` format
- All 8 entries have `priority: 0.6` and `changeFrequency: 'monthly'`
- The return statement spreads `featureCategoryPages` between `featurePages` and `docPages`
- After `pnpm build --filter=@dorkos/site`, visiting `/sitemap.xml` contains 8 entries matching `/features/category/` pattern
- `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- `pnpm lint` passes with zero warnings

tasks.subject: [site-feature-category-pages] [P3] Add Feature Categories section to llms.txt route handler
tasks.description:
Update `apps/site/src/app/llms.txt/route.ts` to add a `## Feature Categories` section after the existing `## Features` section.

The existing file imports from `@/layers/features/marketing/lib/features`:
```typescript
import { features } from '@/layers/features/marketing/lib/features';
```

Update this import to also include `CATEGORY_LABELS` and `FeatureCategory`:
```typescript
import {
  features,
  CATEGORY_LABELS,
  type FeatureCategory,
} from '@/layers/features/marketing/lib/features';
```

Add the following function after the existing `buildFeaturesSection()` function (around line 104):

```typescript
function buildFeatureCategoriesSection(): string {
  return (Object.keys(CATEGORY_LABELS) as FeatureCategory[])
    .map((cat) => {
      const label = CATEGORY_LABELS[cat];
      const catFeatures = features.filter((f) => f.category === cat);
      const lines = catFeatures.map((f) => `- ${f.name}: ${f.tagline}`);
      return `### ${label}\n${lines.join('\n')}`;
    })
    .join('\n\n');
}
```

Then update the template string in the `GET()` function to include the new section after `## Features`. The relevant portion of the template string changes from:

```
## Features

${buildFeaturesSection()}

## Documentation
```

To:

```
## Features

${buildFeaturesSection()}

## Feature Categories

${buildFeatureCategoriesSection()}

## Documentation
```

The `buildFeatureCategoriesSection()` function groups features by category, outputting a `### CategoryLabel` heading followed by bullet lines in `- Feature Name: tagline` format. Each category group is separated by a blank line.

Example output for the chat category:
```
### Chat
- Chat Interface: A web UI for every agent session, with streaming output in real time
- File Uploads: Drop files into the chat — agents read them as context
```

No other changes to `route.ts` are needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- `route.ts` imports `CATEGORY_LABELS` and `FeatureCategory` from `@/layers/features/marketing/lib/features`
- `buildFeatureCategoriesSection()` function is added after `buildFeaturesSection()`
- The function iterates all `CATEGORY_LABELS` keys and groups features by category
- Each group starts with `### {label}` heading
- Each feature line is `- {feature.name}: {feature.tagline}`
- The `## Feature Categories` section appears in the template string between `## Features` and `## Documentation`
- After `pnpm build --filter=@dorkos/site`, visiting `/llms.txt` shows a `## Feature Categories` section with all 8 category groups
- Categories with zero features in the data still produce a heading (no features to list)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors
- `pnpm lint` passes with zero warnings
tasks.activeForm: Adding Feature Categories section to llms.txt

tasks.subject: [site-feature-category-pages] [P4] Validate typecheck, lint, build, and manual browser checks
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite to confirm the implementation is correct. This task is a checklist-style verification — no new code is written unless a failure requires a fix. Run the following commands in order from the repo root: **Step 1: Type checking** ```bash pnpm typecheck ``` Expected: zero errors. If errors exist, fix them before proceeding. **Step 2: Lint** ```bash pnpm lint ``` Expected: zero warnings and zero errors. Fix any issues before proceeding. **Step 3: Build** ```bash pnpm build --filter=@dorkos/site ``` Expected: build succeeds with all 8 category pages rendered. Look for output like: ``` /features/category/chat /features/category/agent-control /features/category/scheduling /features/category/messaging /features/category/integration /features/category/discovery /features/category/visualization /features/category/infrastructure ``` **Step 4: Manual browser checks** With the site running (`pnpm dev` or build output served), verify: - `/features/category/chat` renders 2 feature rows (Chat Interface, File Uploads) with full detail layout - `/features/category/scheduling` renders 1 row: Pulse Scheduler with `` visible - `/features/category/integration` renders 3 rows: Slack Adapter, Telegram Adapter, MCP Server - `/features/category/messaging` renders 1 row: Relay Message Bus with `` visible - `/features/category/visualization` renders 1 row: Mesh Topology Graph with `` visible - `/features/category/nonexistent` returns the Next.js 404 page - Back link `← Features` on each category page navigates to `/features` - `/sitemap.xml` contains 8 entries matching the pattern `/features/category/{category}` at priority 0.6 - `/llms.txt` contains `## Feature Categories` section with 8 category groups (`### Chat`, `### Scheduling`, etc.) - `/features` (catalog) is unaffected — product filter tabs work, feature cards render correctly - `/features/pulse-scheduler` (slug detail page) is unaffected **Step 5: Confirm acceptance criteria** All of the following must be true: - [ ] `/features/category/chat` renders Chat Interface and File Uploads with full detail layout - [ ] `/features/category/scheduling` renders Pulse Scheduler with its placeholder screenshot image - [ ] `/features/category/integration` renders 3 features (Slack Adapter, Telegram Adapter, MCP Server) - [ ] All 8 category values have statically generated routes - [ ] Unknown categories (e.g. `/features/category/nonexistent`) return 404 - [ ] Each page has: title tag, meta description, canonical URL, BreadcrumbList JSON-LD, CollectionPage JSON-LD - [ ] Category pages appear in `/sitemap.xml` at priority 0.6 - [ ] `/llms.txt` has a `## Feature Categories` section with features grouped by category - [ ] Features with `media.screenshot` show the `` tag; features without show the branded dark dot-grid CSS block - [ ] `pulse-scheduler`, `relay-message-bus`, `mesh-topology` have `media` fields in `features.ts` - [ ] Placeholder SVG files exist at `apps/site/public/features/pulse-scheduler.png`, `relay-message-bus.png`, `mesh-topology.png` - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes with zero errors - [ ] `pnpm lint` passes with zero warnings - [ ] Existing `/features/[slug]` and `/features` routes are completely unchanged
